panels.title: CPU / tick
panels.description: Average CPU consumed per tick. limit=20 on shard3.

panels.title: CPU bucket
panels.description: Bucket = CPU savings buffer (max 10000). <2000 = burning into reserve. <500 = critical.

panels.title: GCL
panels.description: Global Control Level — caps the number of rooms we can own.

panels.title: Hostiles (colony+remotes)
panels.description: Total hostile creeps across home rooms + visible remotes. Any non-zero = check now.

panels.title: Storage Δ/tick
panels.description: Sum of net energy flow into storage across owned rooms (sum of throughput). Negative = drawing down.

panels.title: Spawn busy %
panels.description: Spawn utilization across owned rooms (average). 100% = spawn is the bottleneck.

panels.title: Haulers alive
panels.description: Live logistics creeps colony-wide. Compare to per-room target in the Logistics row.

panels.title: Memory bytes
panels.description: JSON.stringify(Memory).length, refreshed every 100 ticks. Soft limit ~2MB on shard3.

panels.title: Market credits
panels.description: Game.market.credits — bottom line. Drops on order placement, rises on settled trades.

panels.title: Room — spawn queue depth & build sites
Spawn busy %
panels.description: Per-room: percent of spawns currently spawning (snapshot). Grafana's avg over a window shows utilisation. Sustained 100% = spawn-bound — add a spawn or reduce churn. Sustained 0% = idle capacity.

panels.title: Tower energy min
panels.description: Per-room: minimum energy among our towers. Below 200 (1 ranged attack) we can't punch anything. Drops on combat ticks; should recover quickly via Filler.

panels.title: Creeps by role (colony-wide)
panels.description: Live creep counts grouped by ownerTag prefix-before-colon (e.g. logistics, worker, mining, filler, queen, construction, upgrade, reserver, remote-source, remote-vision, remote-repair). At-a-glance pool composition without drilling per-process.

panels.title: Throughput — storage rate (e/tick)
panels.description: Net energy flow into storage, averaged over the statsHistory window (~600 ticks). Negative = drawing down.

panels.title: Throughput — upgrade rate (pt/tick)
panels.description: Controller progress per tick, averaged over statsHistory window. RCL-8 cap is 15 pt/t.

panels.title: Throughput — build rate (pt/tick)
panels.description: Construction progress per tick. Drops negative when a site completes (its progress disappears from the sum) — real signal, not a bug.

panels.title: Construction work outstanding
panels.description: Sum of in-flight construction site progress, per room. Goes up when new sites are placed, down as builders work, jumps to 0 when the last site completes.

panels.title: Bunker plan progress %
panels.description: Per-room: percentage of basePlan structures that are currently *allowed at this RCL* AND actually built. Drops at every RCL bump (new slots unlock) and climbs as ConstructionProcess catches up. Plateaus = bunker complete for this RCL tier.

panels.title: TrafficManager intents (snapshot)
panels.description: Count of move intents registered in TrafficManager when StatsProcess writes the segment — i.e. how many creeps are *trying* to move this tick. Sanity check: spike near 0 means everyone is stationary (potential deadlock).

panels.title: TrafficManager swaps + pushes
panels.description: Number of head-on swaps and push-chains the TrafficManager resolved on the last reconcile. High values = busy corridors with bidirectional or cross-priority traffic. Per-tick snapshot — use Grafana's sum over a window for cumulative.

panels.title: Pathfind — calls / cross-room / incomplete
panels.description: PathFinder.search invocations per tick from creep.travelTo. `calls` = total; `crossRoom` = subset that crossed room borders (after Tier 1 multi-room serialization a multi-room trip should produce ~1 call instead of one per border); `incomplete` = calls that hit maxOps without reaching the goal (high values mean we need a higher maxOps or the route is genuinely blocked).

panels.title: Pathfind — ops per tick (≈ CPU × 1000)
panels.description: Sum of `PathFinder.Path.ops` across all searches this tick. Per the API one op is ~0.001 CPU, so opsTotal/1000 ≈ CPU spent in pathfinding alone. A healthy mid-game colony sits well under 5000 ops/tick. Spikes line up with replans (e.g. construction sites added invalidates cached paths via stuck → repath).

panels.title: Haulers — target / alive / pending (per room)
panels.description: HaulerScheduler.optimalHaulerCount (target) vs current alive logistics creeps vs pending spawn queue requests. Big and persistent target>alive gap = pool is under-spawning (check pending: should pile up if energyAvailable is the blocker). target=alive+pending and stable = steady-state. Lines named '<room>.<metric>'.

panels.title: Haulers — distribution (atStorage / loaded / empty)
panels.description: Where the live haulers are RIGHT NOW. atStorage = within 3 tiles of storage (parked / dispatching / dropping off). loaded = carrying any energy (delivering). empty = zero carry (in flight toward pickup OR parked at storage). loaded+empty = alive. atStorage stays high while containers visibly hold energy = wait-for-fill is over-conservative or pool is over-sized; investigate via haulerDiag().

panels.title: HaulerScheduler — cache age (per room) + recomputes/tick
panels.description: Per-room cacheAge = ticks since last full HaulerScheduler.ensureCache rebuild. Should hover near 0 during active construction (C4 + B2 invalidate the cache on home/remote structure changes), and climb toward the 250-tick TTL backstop during steady-state late-game. recomputes = number of rooms that triggered a full rebuild this tick (separate single series); sparse spikes are healthy, a flat ridge means something is invalidating every tick.

panels.title: Remote — reservation countdown
panels.description: controller.reservation.ticksToEnd per remote. Below 1500 ReserverProcess spawns a replacement; below 0 means we lost the reservation (source caps drop to 1500e and decay rate doubles for containers).

panels.title: Remote — most damaged infra HP %
panels.description: Lowest HP percentage across ALL our roads + containers in the remote (0-100). Thresholds match the repair policy: RemoteRoomProcess flips needsRepair when this drops below 75%; RemoteRepairProcess targets 90% on each structure. "No data" = remote not visible this tick (we don't have a creep there to enumerate structures).

panels.title: Remote — creep pool per role
panels.description: Live creep counts per role per remote. reserver should be 1 most of the time; miner = containers built; vision = 0 once reserver/miner provide visibility; repairer = 1 while needsRepair flag is set.

panels.title: Remote — container energy queue (waiting for haulers)
panels.description: Sum of energy sitting in remote source containers per remote. Steady oscillation between 0 and ~1600 is normal (miner deposits, hauler drains). Sustained climb to ~2000 (cap) means the hauler pool is undersized for this remote's travelTicks.

panels.title: Remote — build progress (containers + roads done / planned)
panels.description: Counts of sources whose container is built and whose roads are fully laid, per remote. Both should reach `sourceCount` shortly after enableRemote; until they do RemoteSourceProcess holds off spawning miners and HaulerScheduler excludes the container from the pool.

panels.title: Remote — needsRepair flag
panels.description: Binary 0/1 per remote — 1 means RemoteRoomProcess saw a road/container below 75% HP. RemoteRepairProcess spawns a 200e repairer (1W/1C/1M) while the flag is set; flag drops when everything's back above 75%.

panels.title: Hostile body parts per room
panels.description: ATTACK + RANGED_ATTACK + HEAL + WORK part counts across hostile creeps per room. Rough threat assessment: small numbers = invader / scout; large = a player raid.

panels.title: Remote — hostile creep count
panels.description: Number of hostile creeps in each visible remote (visibility-gated — "no data" when the remote isn't observed). Counts include SK creeps (Keepers + invader cores) when applicable; for ordinary reserved remotes any non-zero is a real raid signal. Defenders only react to home-room hostiles, so this is the primary watch for remote attacks.

panels.title: Remote — hostile body parts (per remote)
panels.description: ATTACK + RANGED_ATTACK + HEAL + WORK part counts across hostile creeps per visible remote. Stacked per remote so the bar height = total threat for that remote. Mirrors the home-room "Hostile body parts per room" panel — visibility-gated, omitted when the remote isn't observed.

panels.title: Route version — counter & mutation rate
panels.description: Memory.colony.routeVersion is a monotonic counter bumped by ScoutProcess every time route-affecting intel changes (sealedExits flips, dangerousOwned toggle). Read by cached paths (GoState.routeVersion) and HaulerScheduler haulerSignature — mismatch invalidates the cache. Series A: current value (stair-step line). Series B: rate of change per minute, via nonNegativeDerivative + scaleToSeconds — spikes line up with scout discoveries; near-zero = intel stable.

panels.title: Scout — alive / pending / queue depth
panels.description: Scout creep population: alive count + pending spawn requests, plus the BFS queue of rooms still to visit. Healthy: alive ≥ 1 when queueDepth > 0; pending settles to 0 once the scout body lands. Sustained queueDepth >> 0 with alive=0 → spawn-side problem.

panels.title: Scout — intel count, stale, oldest age
panels.description: intelCount: total rooms ever scouted. intelStaleCount: rooms past ScoutProcess.RESCAN (20000 ticks ≈ 20h on shard3) — should be small in steady state, growth means scout falls behind refill cadence. oldestIntelAge: worst-case staleness across the whole map. Reaching RoutePolicy.STALE_INTEL_TICKS (100k ≈ 100h) triggers soft-block downgrade in routeCost. unreachableCount: rooms scout tried + failed (travelTo ERR_NO_PATH) within the last UNREACHABLE_BACKOFF=50000 ticks. Skipped from enqueue until the window expires.

panels.title: Sealed intel — rooms sealed / dangerous / sides fully blocked
panels.description: roomsSealed: scouted rooms with at least one sealed exit tile (terrain wall or hostile rampart blocking interior). roomsDangerous: rooms with dangerousOwned flag (hostile owner + ≥1 active tower). sidesFullyBlocked: count of room-sides where EVERY exit tile is sealed — these reject at the route layer (routeCost = Infinity). Per-tile partially-sealed sides only block at PathFinder layer (PathFinder finds an open segment).

panels.title: Sealed tiles — total + per side (N/S/W/E)
panels.description: totalSealedTiles: sum of "1" bits across all rooms × all sides — total barricade density on the carved map. bySide.{N,S,W,E}: how many sides on each compass direction are fully blocked. Asymmetry hints at where the abandoned bases sit.

panels.title: Route policy — decisions per tick (allowed / soft / blocked)
panels.description: Per-tick decisions from RoutePolicy.routeCost (the routeCallback fed to Game.map.findRoute). allowed: cost 1.0 baseline. softBlocked: cost SOFT_BLOCK_COST=20 (stale-sealed side — try if no alternative). blocked: POSITIVE_INFINITY (hostile-owned tower, SK, fresh-sealed side). Stacked to show the rejection share; rate-of-change (irate over 5m) shows how active routing is right now.

panels.title: Route overlay — applySealedExits calls & tiles marked
panels.description: Per-tick from RoutePolicy.applySealedExits: overlay.calls = how many PathFinder roomCallbacks marked ≥1 sealed tile, tilesApplied = sum of those marks. Ratio tiles/calls is the average per-room sealed density when PathFinder is forced to consider a sealed corridor. Zero means no PathFinder.search ran through a known-sealed room this tick.
